text: The screen displays instructions to dissect through the fat layer to expose the vessel wall. The surgeon uses scissors to scrape off residual fatty tissue from the anterior surface of the vessel without energy application. Once the vessel is exposed, the surgeon uses a grasper to perform bipolar coagulation at two indicated points until the vessel turns white, signaling satisfactory coagulation. The exercise then prompts cutting the vessel between the coagulation points, while avoiding injury or excessive tension.
